name: Brennan Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The Riemann sphere (extended complex plane) with its compactification at infinity, containing all possible analytic continuations of a germ of holomorphic functions.

VINIT.justification: The initial uninitialized state: a single point z₀ with a power series expansion, before any extension or continuation is attempted.

TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor that contains all possible analytic continuations; the complete boundary within which all extensions must remain.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the directed transformation of the germ along a curve γ in the complex plane, extending the domain of definition.

AREV.justification: Reverse morphism: the descent back through a branch point, revealing multi-valuedness and the non-trivial topology of the analytic continuation.

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining: if γ₁ and γ₂ are two continuation paths, their composition γ₁ ∘ γ₂ yields a single extended analytic function.

IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference: the continuation along a contractible loop that returns to the same germ, proving the function is single-valued on that region.

FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: at a branch point, the single-valued germ splits into multiple distinct branches (e.g., √z splits into two sheets at z=0).

FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: the multiple branches are fused back into a single multi-valued analytic function on the Riemann surface, recovering the original continuation structure.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch: the continuation path avoids all singularities and branch points; the function extends holomorphically along the entire path.

EVALF.justification: Negative branch: the continuation path encounters a pole, essential singularity, or natural boundary; extension fails or becomes multi-valued.

ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: at a branch point, both sheets of the Riemann surface are held simultaneously without resolution; the function is both single-valued and multi-valued.

IFIX.chosen_element: permanent analytic continuation record (Riemann surface topology)
IFIX.justification: Irreversible fixation: once the Riemann surface is constructed and the monodromy group is determined, the analytic continuation structure is permanently recorded and cannot be undone.

phase_2.split_input: germ of single-valued holomorphic function f at z₀
split_outputs: branch A: analytic continuation along path γ₁ (e.g., upper sheet of √z)
branch B: analytic continuation along path γ₂ (e.g., lower sheet of √z)
phase_2.fuse_result: multi-valued analytic function on Riemann surface, which when restricted to any single sheet recovers the original germ f

phase_3.void_description: 00 — uninitialized: no germ, no domain, no analytic structure yet defined
phase_3.true_description: 01 — affirmative: analytic continuation successful, function extends holomorphically, single-valued on the chosen path
phase_3.false_description: 10 — negative: continuation obstructed, singularity encountered, or multi-valuedness revealed at branch point
phase_3.both_description: 11 — paradice: monodromy state where both branches coexist simultaneously on the Riemann surface without resolution into a single value

steps.domain_action: genesis of a germ f(z) = Σ aₙ(z - z₀)ⁿ at a single point z₀ in the complex plane
establish the Riemann sphere ℂ ∪ {∞} as the universal boundary containing all possible continuations
begin analytic continuation along a path γ from z₀ toward a target point w
compose continuation steps along γ into a single extended function on the path
verify that a contractible loop returns to the same germ (identity continuation, single-valuedness)
encounter a branch point (e.g., z = 0 for √z); the germ splits into two distinct analytic branches
T-arm — continuation along upper sheet avoids the singularity, extends successfully
F-arm — continuation along lower sheet reveals the multi-valued nature, monodromy non-trivial
at the branch point itself, both sheets are held simultaneously; the function is both single-valued (locally) and multi-valued (globally)
reverse morphism — trace back through the branch point, descending into the multi-valued structure
glue the two branches together on the Riemann surface; the multi-valued function is now single-valued on the surface
verify that a loop around the branch point on the Riemann surface returns to the same point (monodromy group element)
permanently record the Riemann surface topology and monodromy group; the analytic continuation structure is now fixed and irreversible
compose all continuation paths into the complete analytic continuation of f
the final extended function is contained within the Riemann sphere; all singularities and branch points are now accounted for

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Weierstrass analytic continuation algorithm: translates a germ and a path into a sequence of power series expansions along the path
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Monodromy group: components (sheets of the Riemann surface) communicate via the action of loops around branch points
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Riemann surface: the multi-valued function is stored as a single-valued function on a topologically non-trivial surface
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Path ordering: the order in which continuation paths are traversed determines the monodromy and the final Riemann surface structure
phase_5.alfs_store: Cauchy integral formula and residue theorem: the canonical bootstrap store for computing analytic continuations and verifying holomorphicity

phase_6.cycle_cost: Topological complexity: each branch point adds a generator to the monodromy group; the cost is the rank of the fundamental group of the punctured domain
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Germ at z₀: a single power series with finite radius of convergence
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Riemann surface: a complete analytic continuation with all branch points resolved and monodromy group determined
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The Brennan theorem preserves the analytic structure: information is neither created nor destroyed, only reorganized topologically on the Riemann surface. The total 'complexity' (monodromy group rank) is invariant under different continuation paths.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → ENGAGR → AREV → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → IFIX → CLINK → TANCH
-- Class: Brennan Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(9,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=15
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 10)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [9] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [10] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [11] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑭  | identity — self-imscription
--   [12] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑠 → 𐑱  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [13] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑭 → 𐑡  | composition — regime coherence
--   [14] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑱 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def brennan_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (genesis of a germ f(z) = Σ aₙ(z - z₀)ⁿ at a single point z₀ in the complex plane)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (establish the Riemann sphere ℂ ∪ {∞} as the universal boundary containing all...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (begin analytic continuation along a path γ from z₀ toward a target point w)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose continuation steps along γ into a single extended function on the path)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [4] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (verify that a contractible loop returns to the same germ (identity continuati...)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (encounter a branch point (e.g., z = 0 for √z); the germ splits into two distinct analytic branches) / FFUSE [10] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (T-arm — continuation along upper sheet avoids the singularity, extends succes...)
      -- F-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (F-arm — continuation along lower sheet reveals the multi-valued nature, monod...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (at the branch point itself, both sheets are held simultaneously; the function...)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (reverse morphism — trace back through the branch point, descending into the m...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (glue the two branches together on the Riemann surface; the multi-valued function is now single-valued on the surface)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [11] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (verify that a loop around the branch point on the Riemann surface returns to ...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑠 𐑱)  -- [12] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (permanently record the Riemann surface topology and monodromy group; the anal...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑭 𐑡)  -- [13] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose all continuation paths into the complete analytic continuation of f)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑱 𐑼)  -- [14] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (the final extended function is contained within the Riemann sphere; all singu...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def brennan_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (brennan_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def brennan_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (brennan_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem brennan_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
